courses.description: This course is an introductory course for all students which aims to provide an overview on the history, basic concepts and current issues in the field of public health. Students will gain a broad, general understanding on how public health issues can be framed in the three interrelated domains of health protection, health improvement, and health services. This foundation course will equip students with an analytic lens that is essential for deeper learning in each of the three domains.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
For taught postgraduate students in:- 
(i) PgD Health Services Management and Public Health; (ii) PgD Public Health; (iii) MSc Health Education; (iv) Master of Public Health; or 
(v) MBA Health Care
courses.learning_outcomes: Understand the basic concept and characteristics of public health and social determinants of health. Understand the key functions of health system in analyzing public health issues; Understand the basic concept of population, society and global disparities of health and promoting equity; Understand the basic structures of health systems, in particular the challenges of NCDs & communicable diseases; Gain familiarity of concepts of environmental change and global health.

courses.subject: PBHT
courses.title: Introduction to Qualitative Research: Theories and Research Design
courses.description: This qualitative methods course will introduce students to the topical issues related to conducting qualitative research and allow for hands-on exposure to the methods of data collection and analysis used in this methodology
courses.learning_outcomes:
Student should be able to:

* Understand the definition and features of qualitative research
* Understand the philosophical basis of qualitative approaches and describe its applications
* Understand and apply the skills of conducting qualitative research: case study, observation, interview, focus group, and textual analysis
* Understand and apply the skills of data coding and interpretation in the analytical proce
courses.course_syllabus:
| Introduction to qualitative methods Study design and Sampling |
| --- |
| Case Study & Observation |
| In-depth interviews Interview guide development |
| Interview & Observation skills development & practice |
| Focus group |
| Inductive and deductive textual analysis |
| Data analysis Coding |
| Qualitative writing |
| Final Exam |
courses.required_readings: Pope, Catherine; Mays, Nicholas (Eds.) (2020): Qualitative research in health care. Fourth edition. Hoboken: Wiley-Blackwell. (Ebook version is available via CUHK library.)
courses.recommended_readings:
1. Qualitative Research Methods

* Braun, V., & Clarke, V. (2013). Successful qualitative research: A practical guide for beginners. sage.
* Creswell, J. W., & Poth, C. N. (2016). Qualitative inquiry and research design: Choosing among five approaches. Sage publications. Designing Qualitative Research
* Flick, U., von Kardoff, E., & Steinke, I. (Eds.). (2004). A companion to qualitative research. Sage.
* Hansen, E. C. (2020). Successful qualitative health research: a practical introduction. Routledge.

1. Grounded Theory

* Corbin, J., & Strauss, A. (2014). Basics of qualitative research: Techniques and procedures for developing grounded theory. Sage publications.
* Foley, G., & Timonen, V. (2015). Using grounded theory method to capture and analyze health care experiences. Health services research, 50(4), 1195-1210.

1. Interview

* Gubrium, J. F., Holstein, J. A., Marvasti, A. B., & McKinney, K. D. (Eds.). (2012). The SAGE handbook of interview research: The complexity of the craft. Sage Publications.

1. Focus Group

* Krueger, R. A. (2014). Focus groups: A practical guide for applied research. Sage publications.

1. Textual Analysis

* McKee, A. (2003). Textual analysis: A beginner's guide. Sage.
* Hsieh, H. F., & Shannon, S. E. (2005). Three approaches to qualitative content analysis. Qualitative health research, 15(9), 1277-1288.

1. Data Analysis

* Braun, V., & Clarke, V. (2006). Using thematic analysis in psychology. Qualitative research in psychology, 3(2), 77-101.
* Gale, N. K., Heath, G., Cameron, E., Rashid, S., & Redwood, S. (2013). Using the framework method for the analysis of qualitative data in multi-disciplinary health research. BMC medical research methodology, 13(1), 1-8.

1. Research Ethics

* Hammersley, M., & Traianou, A. (2012). Ethics in qualitative research: Controversies and contexts. Sage.
* Richards, H. M., & Schwartz, L. J. (2002). Ethics of qualitative research: are there special issues for health services research?. Family practice, 19(2), 135-139.9.

1. Others

* Creswell, J. W., & Creswell, J. D. (2017). Research design: Qualitative, quantitative, and mixed methods approaches. Sage publications.
* Duarte, N. (2008). Slide: ology: The art and science of creating great presentations (Vol. 1). Sebastapol, CA: O’Reilly Media.

courses.description: This course is a basic introduction to the human body for public health students without a bio-medical background. It is geared towards students with an undergraduate degree in the social sciences, arts and physical sciences. It will introduce the overview of basic scientific mechanisms, concepts and principles in health and in the pathogenesis of disease. It provides clinical scientific foundation for applications to the practice of public health. Professionals in the clinical health field such as physicians, nurses, dentists and allied health specialists will not be permitted to take this course for credit.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Public Health
courses.learning_outcomes: This course is a basic introduction to the human body for public health students without a bio-medical background. It provides clinical scientific foundation for applications to the practice of public health. Professionals in the clinical health field such as physicians, nurses, dentists and allied health specialists will not be permitted to take this course for credit.

courses.subject: PBHT
courses.title: R Analytics in Environmental Health Sciences
courses.description: This course provides an introduction to the R programming environment and commonly used tidyverse packages for data preprocessing and visualization. It will also cover basic applications of biostatistics and modeling packages in R. Students will be assessed through an open-book in-class examination covering the skills taught in the course.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Public Health and MSc Epidemiology and Biostatistics
courses.learning_outcomes:
1) Understand basic coding in R computing environment

2) Apply modern coding concepts and practices through the tidyverse packages

3) Process raw data to prepare it for statistical analysis

4) Present and discuss results using the most suitable visualization and interactive methods

5) Apply the coding skills learned in the course to their own projects

6) Have the capability to independently learn and apply R packages not covered in the course
courses.course_syllabus:
| 1. Overview of R and RStudio software |
| --- |
| 2. Reproducibility in science |
| 3. Selecting and filtering data |
| 4. Summarizing data |
| 5. Data cleaning |
| 6. Data manipulation and interactive exploration |
| 7. Data visualization and introduction to factors |
| 8. Introduction to biostatistics |
| 9. Final assessment |
courses.recommended_readings:
1) R for Data Science (2e). https://r4ds.hadley.nz

2) Statistical Inference via Data Science. https://moderndive.com/index.html

3) Data Science for Environmental Health. https://daseh.org/materials\_schedule.html

4) Introduction to Data Science. http://rafalab.dfci.harvard.edu/dsbook/

5) Decoding the exposome: data science methodologies and implications in exposome-wide association studies (ExWASs) https://academic.oup.com/exposome/article/4/1/osae001/7574628

courses.subject: PBHT
courses.title: International Exchange Study
courses.description: Public Health in Action in The Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN): Field study of Brunei Darussalam’s healthcare system, disease prevention and control strategies
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Public Health and MSc Epidemiology & Biostatistics
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. Observe the organisation and delivery of healthcare services in Brunei, and compare them with health service models in other Asia Pacific countries and jurisdictions (including Hong Kong and Mainland China).
2. Explain environmental health and vector control strategies used for vector borne diseases and other infectious diseases.
3. Assess regulatory mechanisms for food safety and worker safety through relevant agencies.
4. Discuss how intersectoral collaboration supports national public health goals, including cross-agency coordination in infectious disease control, environmental health, and safety regulation.
5. Reflect on lessons learned and how insights from Brunei’s public health strategies can inform public health practice in the region.
courses.course_syllabus:
| 1. Healthcare system and services in Brunei Darussalam |
| --- |
| 2. Environmental health & Disease prevention and control |
| 3. Health Promotion, Food Safety & occupational safety regulations |
courses.recommended_readings:
Brunei PPKK, MOH, SHENA, BDFA, JPMC webpages:

<https://ppkk.gov.bn/SitePages/Home.aspx>

<https://moh.gov.bn/>

<https://jpmcbrunei.com/>

<https://shena.gov.bn/>

https://bdfa.gov.bn/

courses.subject: PBHT
courses.title: Global Experiential Learning
courses.description: Short-term internships at international organisations: Experiential learning by participating field activities
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Public Health
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. Observe the organisation and delivery of public health related programme and projects in Asia-Pacific region.
2. Supplement their academic knowledge with a practical work experience in a field related to the work of hosting organization.
3. Assist in relevant fields in connection to the hosting organization’s strategic framework and to UN Sustainable Development Goals.
4. Participate in high-level discussion on how intersectoral collaboration supports including cross-agency coordination in infectious disease control, One Health, and food safety regulation.
5. Reflect on lessons learned and how insights from Brunei’s public health strategies can inform public health practice in the region.
courses.course_syllabus:
| 1. Understanding of organizational mandates and programmes |
| --- |
| 2. Organizational assignments |
| 3. Health Promotion, Food Safety & One Health |
courses.required_readings: To be confirmed by course coordinator
courses.recommended_readings: To be confirmed by course coordinator

courses.subject: PBHT
courses.title: Global and Regional Perspectives on Noncommunicable Diseases Prevention and Control
courses.description: This course aims to provide students an in-depth understanding of the burden of noncommunicable diseases (NCDs) at the local, regional, and global scales. It equips students with the knowledge and tools to prevent and control NCDs and associated risk factors using effective public health strategies, as well as how to conduct NCD surveillance and research to assess and adapt interventions across diverse population settings.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Public Health
courses.learning_outcomes:
After the course, students will be able to
1. describe the NCD burden globally, regionally, and locally, and factors influencing the patterns and trajectories in different population settings.
2. explain common risk factors and determinants of NCDs, and interactions among biological, social, environmental, and modifiable behavioral factors.
3. identify public health strategies, including population and targeted interventions, to address NCDs and risk factors in various populations.
4. discuss success factors and lessons from case studies of population interventions for preventing and controlling high-burden NCDs.
5. demonstrate understanding of common NCD surveillance and research methods, and design a research study proposal for NCD surveillance in a population.
courses.course_syllabus:
| Lecture 1 - NCD burden, risk factors and determinants |
| --- |
| Lecture 2 - Population based approaches to the prevention and control of NCDs |
| Lecture 3 - Case studies on high burden and emerging NCDs |
| Lecture 4 - NCD surveillance and research methodologies |
| Lecture 5 - Preparation of written assignment |
courses.required_readings:
Noncommunicable Diseases, Health Topics, World Health Organisation.
https://www.who.int/health-topics/noncommunicable-diseases#tab=tab\_1
